name: meshio (Mesh File I/O for Scientific Computing)

performance.notes: I/O-bound; speed proportional to mesh size; HDF5-based formats fast

agent_readiness.notes: Universal mesh file I/O for 40+ formats. import meshio. mesh = meshio.read('mesh.vtk'). mesh.points. mesh.cells. mesh.point_data. mesh.cell_data. meshio.write('output.xdmf', mesh). Convert: meshio convert input.vtk output.msh. Formats: VTK, VTU, XDMF/HDF5, Gmsh, Abaqus, CGNS, Exodus, MEDIT, OBJ, STL, PLY, etc. CLI: meshio info mesh.vtk. mesh.remove_lower_dimensional_cells(). mesh.remove_orphaned_nodes(). Used in FEM pre/post-processing.

gotchas: Some format conversions lose data (mesh-specific fields not universally transferable)
Format-specific limitations (Gmsh partition data, boundary tags) require attention
STL has no point data — only geometry
Large meshes (>10M elements) may be slow to convert
best_when: Converting between FEM mesh formats (VTK ↔ Gmsh ↔ XDMF ↔ Abaqus)
When 40+ format support is needed from a single library
CLI mesh conversion without writing Python code
avoid_when: Large-scale parallel mesh processing (use parallel HDF5 libraries)
When only one specific format is needed (use format-specific library)
summary: meshio provides universal mesh I/O for 40+ formats. meshio.read('mesh.vtk') returns Mesh object with .points, .cells, .point_data. meshio.write('output.msh', mesh). CLI: meshio convert input.vtk output.xdmf. Formats: VTK, Gmsh, XDMF/HDF5, Abaqus, CGNS, STL, OBJ, PLY. MIT. Best for FEM mesh format conversion and pre/post-processing pipelines.
